extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r2, whose guard is the conjunction of 12 inputs including polypharmacy. rec_drug_b is set true by r3 whose guard is just polypharmacy. No rule ever sets either output false (defaults only apply when unset). Therefore any input that satisfies r2 necessarily satisfies r3 (since polypharmacy is one of r2's required conjuncts), forcing both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true. Setting all 12 of r2's conjuncts true (polypharmacy, elderly, immunosuppressed, hepatic_impairment, prior_gi_bleed, g6pd_deficiency, qt_prolongation, pregnancy, seizure_history, hypertension, anticoagulated, diabetes) makes r2 fire (rec_drug_a=true) and r3 fire (rec_drug_b=true), so (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) is jointly true, violating mutual exclusion. The remaining inputs (heart_failure, renal_impairment, bradycardia) only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; set false.
